reviews.review_description: The best vr game i played,good concept,  and not dizzyness inducing.
Yep

reviews.review_title: SWARM2 is Not a Sequel, It’s a Downgrade
reviews.review_description:
First I wanna say I never leave reviews on anything!!!! Iv left a 5/5 review on the original SWARM because it’s the best game on VR but SWARM 2 is a big disappointment!! As someone who loved the original SWARM, I was excited for this. After playing for hours, I can confidently say this game feels like a massive step backward in every way.

The movement mechanics are completely changed. In the first game, you had to physically pull down to swing yourself, which made it feel intense and skill-based. Now you move using the left thumbstick, which makes the game playable sitting down. That completely defeats the purpose. SWARM was described as “Spider-Man with guns” for a reason. This is not Spider-Man. This is something much slower and much less exciting. As well as it just does not feel as smooth as the original game in terms of movement. Sure I like you can grapple on more things such as buildings , and you don’t die if you touch the ground in this game but those are things that made the first one exciting!!! Why ruin something that was perfect!!!!! Smh 

As well as the progression system is watered down in this SWARM 2……. In SWARM 1, you had five full zones, each with six unique levels and a different exciting difficult boss at the end. In SWARM 2, I hit a boss on level four and it was the same one over and over again. No zone system, no variety, just repetition.

The health system is frustrating. In the original game, you started every round with full health, which gave each run a fair shot. In this one, your health carries over from round to round, and when you die, you don’t restart the current round, you start from the very beginning. This makes it feel less like a skill challenge and more like a punishment loop. It completely kills motivation to push forward.       
Weapon variety is also disappointing. I’ve only come across two alternate weapons,  a lightning gun and a grenade launcher which were in the original game . That’s it. No real incentive to explore or look forward to power-ups. In the original game they would just spawn and higher zones got you cooler weapons… 

The few mildly interesting mechanic is the perk system, which lets you make upgrades and choose modifiers. BUT THEY ONLY LAST FOR THAT SINGLE RUN , once you die , they are gone . That’s the one thing that adds some freshness, but it’s not enough to carry the rest of the game. As well as they finally added a store for the shards that you collect to increase your health or shield, however again. This is not enough to carry the rest of the game…… 

Overall, this does not feel like a sequel. It feels like a stripped down version of what made the original great. The pacing is worse. The content is thinner. The challenge feels unfair instead of fun. INSTEAD OF LOOKING FORWARD TO HITTING THE RETRY BUTTON LIKE IN THE FIRST GAME, YOU DREADD IT IN THIS GAME! It’s like the game was redesigned to be more casual and accessible at the cost of everything that made it special.

Unless they completely overhaul the progression, boss variety, and restore the fast-paced skill-based gameplay, I cannot recommend this to anyone who enjoyed the original. SWARM 1 is still the better game in every way.
fun fast-paced arcade action
it's like your spider man but with guns

reviews.review_title: Amazing game, just like the first swarm!
reviews.review_description:
Swarm 2 builds on Swarm 1, keeping cool features like weekly challenges and leaderboards. The new roguelike mode adds more fun and makes me want to play again and again. Also, Swarm 2 has better graphics and smoother gameplay (and the future update planned to improve the graphics for the quest 3 will only improve this) than the first game. It's more immersive and fun, so fans should definitely check it out.

One thing I'd like to see improved is when swinging around, I very often become a pinball and bounce around several times. When this happens it throws me off and end up losing my orientation. I didn't have this issue with swarm 1, but hopefully this will be improved with future updates.

Another thing I am disappointed about is that there is no Oculus Link (PC version) included with the standalone version. I loved playing the first swarm game from my PC and was disappointed that there isn't a PC version for this one.

reviews.review_title: Such a stupid small thing ruins the game
reviews.review_description: It's awesome. Way better than the first one.  The only thing is you have to start the game a 2nd time in order to see all the levels and modes it has...which are a lot!.  My only real complaint is that you cannot mute the missile incoming voice for some very strange reason.  The game already flashes a missile warning right in front of your face.  The voice is just super annoying and repetitive. I really hope they allow us to mute this in a future update.

developer_response.body:
**THIS ISSUE HAS BEEN RESOLVED**

Hey Ender772, thanks for reaching out! Sorry the missile incoming noise wasn't mutable when you tried it, that's super annoying. 

We heard you and our dev team has pushed a fix which makes this sound able to be turned off. In the audio settings tab there is a specific button for it under "missile indicator".

Hope this helps!

reviews.review_title: IS THE BLADE OF DESTINY EVER COMING OUT?
reviews.review_description: Simple question.
Can we see how these mods work? I’d like to get the high score in the weekly challenge. Please check the weekly high score. Let us know what mods are being used. Thank you.

I enjoyed SWARM 1 so much! 
I think it was a great game, that made me buy swarm2 without a second thought.

Some mechanics got better… but i didn’t like the “RogueLike” at all… were the whole game is a 1hr Run, but you have to repeat over and over again, buy upgrades and repeat AGAIN…

Also the RNG that spawns enemies sometimes gets Nasty, spawns tons of difficult foes around you and you are dead before you even know what happened
Amazing swinging mechanics and the rogue lite elements are a nice touch. Highly recommend!!!

The original is my all-time favorite VR game, and I absolutely love it.  Had I not played the original, I might think this is a great game.   Sadly, I have seen the potential that this game can be, and it falls short.  

Nearly everything is worse.  I expected a faster and bigger experience, but it's the opposite.  Clunky and slow.  The original gave an indication of which direction the missiles are coming from. In this game, it's impossible to know where the missiles are  coming from, so expect to die randomly.  Sound effects and music are also huge downgrades.  You can no longer launch yourself high into the air,  I'm almost always shooting up now 😒  Worst part is no save points, so if you die, you have to start from the beginning, which becomes tedious playing the same levels over and over.  

If you never played Swarm before start with the original,  if you enjoyed the original, I would recommend sitting this one out and hope they fix Swarm 3.
Really feels like I'm spiderman but with guns love it loads. A very action packed game and I 100% recommend it!

reviews.review_title: Best Game sequel of all time
reviews.review_description:
Ever since I got Swarm I’ve really enjoyed a lot like it’s really a fun action shooter game. If you Enjoy Swarm Then Swarm 2 should be the best game for you if you wanna have more fun and action. This really changed the whole game experience for everyone and It’s really enjoyable.
Credits to the developers.

The original was one of the most amazing VR games and still is to date... This is a travesty and I have no idea how this was approved.


Every single map feels cramped there's no verticality to anything. The levels feel small all you do is bounce off of all the objects then instantly off the invisible walls that surround you. I wouldn't feel comfortable paying $5 for this mess. 

Shameful sequel at best. The levels should be three times the size they are and they shouldn't be filled with remnants of buildings that have nothing to grip on to. This whole game relies on you to use the thumb stick instead of the swinging mechanic which makes the game great. 

Oh well back to the original. please don't buy this

developer_response.body:
Hey Dayn, thanks for the feedback. Sorry Swarm 2 didn't live up to your expectations. As you play through Swarm 2, you'll unlock new levels with each run through roguelike. Some of them are tighter, and rely on your movement skills to navigate through. Others are more open, and have more of the verticality you mentioned. We also just released an update with 4 new maps, I hope you check them out!

We did this to ensure that both newcomers and veteran players had something challenging but rewarding. Open levels are easier to get a hang of, but we heard from experienced Swarm players that they wanted more compact levels that required more skill to maneuver. 

All of the maps in Swarm 2 are much, much bigger than in Swarm 1, and almost all surfaces should be grippable - if you find one that isn't - please email support@greenskygamesstudios.com, as it may be a glitch!

If you'd like some tips on movement, head over to discord.gg/greensky - we've got staff and experienced players that would love to help you out!

reviews.review_description: This game is probably the most fun i ever have playing on my quest. If you haven’t got it, do yourself a favor and play it. You’re missing out.

I played this game and loved the new features. For example, I like how you can grapple buildings and other objects. I also feel like the dash option is a nice added feature. I still have to play more, but so far I am really enjoying it.

reviews.review_title: Back in the swing of it
reviews.review_description:
What an absolute gem of a sequel. Also of everything from the first game and blows it out the water. 
 There’s plenty more to do in this follow up. Including trial mode, arcade mode and free roam. 

Cannot recommend  this game enough. GET IT.
Swinging mechanics are surprisingly very smooth and don't cause you to be nauseous. Fun game mechanics, great visual style that doesn't get boring. Nicely done. A must have for sure!

The swinging mechanic is very fun and the roguelike take on it is very epic and cool, the gameplay is very fun and the introduction of new mechanics every time adds a lot to the gameplay. So yeah definitely buy it
